<|file_sep|>README.md
# Tokenizer

This is a simple tokenizer that can be used to replicate the behavior of the GPT-2 tokenizer as well as the GPT-3 and GPT-4 tokenizers, which are largely similar but use different vocabularies and have larger vocabularies.

This tokenizer is designed to be straightforward and understandable, and to be usable in many different programming languages. It is not designed to be particularly fast.

## Usage

See the [usage example](usage_example.py) for how to use the tokenizer.

## Files

- `tokenizer.py`: The tokenizer itself.
- `vocab.bpe`: The vocabulary of merges.
- `vocab.json`: The vocabulary of tokens.
- `usage_example.py`: An example of how to use the tokenizer.

## License

MIT

<|file_sep|>README.md
# Multilingual Text Classification

This repository contains the code and resources for the paper "Multilingual Text Classification: A Comprehensive Benchmark and Analysis".

## Overview

This project provides a comprehensive benchmark for multilingual text classification, covering 100 languages and 12 tasks. The repository includes:

- Datasets for 100 languages
- Code for training and evaluating models
- Pre-trained models and results

## Installation

To install the required dependencies, run:

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

## Usage

### Data Preparation

The datasets are available in the `data/` directory. Each dataset is organized by language and task.

### Training

To train a model, use the following command:

```bash
python train.py --data_dir data/ --output_dir models/ --task <task_name> --language <language_code>
```

### Evaluation

To evaluate a trained model, use:

```bash
python evaluate.py --model_dir models/ --task <task_name> --language <language_code>
```

## Results

The results of our experiments are available in the `results/` directory. Each file contains the performance metrics for a specific task and language.

## Citation

If you use this benchmark in your research, please cite our paper:

```
@article{multilingual_text_classification,
  title={Multilingual Text Classification: A Comprehensive Benchmark and Analysis},
  author={Your Name and Others},
  journal={Journal Name},
  year={2024}
}
```

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License.

# Multilingual Text Classification

This repository contains the code and resources for the paper "Multilingual Text Classification: A Comparative Study of Transformer Models and Traditional Machine Learning Approaches".

## Overview

This project explores the performance of various machine learning and deep learning models for multilingual text classification. The study compares traditional machine learning approaches with state-of-the-art transformer models across multiple languages.

## Repository Structure

- `code/`: Contains the implementation code for the experiments
- `data/`: Dataset information and preprocessing scripts
- `results/`: Experimental results and analysis
- `models/`: Trained model checkpoints (if applicable)

## Requirements

- Python 3.8+
- PyTorch
- Transformers (Hugging Face)
- Scikit-learn
- Other dependencies listed in `requirements.txt`

## Installation

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

## Usage

1. Data Preparation
2. Model Training
3. Evaluation

(Detailed usage instructions will be added)

## Citation

If you use this code in your research, please cite our paper:

```
@article{yourpaper,
  title={Multilingual Text Classification: A Comparative Study of Transformer Models and Traditional Machine Learning Approaches},
  author={Your Name et al.},
  journal={Your Journal},
  year={2024}
}
```

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the LICENSE file for details.

## Contact

For questions or issues, please open an issue in this repository.
